prompt: In a good powerlifting program, how much training time should be hypertrophy-focused, and how should that change with training age and in-season vs off-season? Also, when is someone strong enough to start bodybuilding-style accessory work?
assertions: States newer lifters generally need more hypertrophy work, not less
Recommends learning the lifts first, then building muscle while maintaining heavier work
Describes shift toward more specific strength work as lifters advance and hypertrophy slows
Notes high-rep bodybuilding work is a poor fit close to competition (within ~4 weeks)
Recommends hypertrophy blocks in off-season, especially when moving up or filling out a weight class
